data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Amazon uses a microservices architecture to handle its vast and varied operations, allowing independent service deployment and scalability.
data.technical_spec: Services are decoupled and communicate over REST APIs.

data.title: Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2)
data.description: Central to Amazon's architecture, EC2 provides scalable computing capacity.
data.technical_spec: Supports auto-scaling, virtual server instances, and load balancing.

data.title: Amazon Simple Storage Service (S3)
data.description: S3 offers object storage with scalability, data availability, security, and performance.
data.technical_spec: Supports REST APIs, governed by AWS IAM policies.

data.title: API Gateway
data.description: Amazon uses API Gateway to manage the publishing, maintenance, and security of APIs.
data.technical_spec: Handles authentication, authorization, and usage metering of APIs.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Amazon CloudFront accelerates delivery of your content to users.
data.technical_spec: Provides caching of content, SSL support, and detailed analytics.
